subject: Physics
title: Maxwell's Demon
description: Learn Maxwell's Demon in one minute.

hook.text: James Clerk Maxwell imagined a tiny demon that could sort fast and slow molecules—apparently violating the second law.

buildup.text: The demon sits at a door between two gas chambers, letting fast molecules through one way and slow the other.

discovery.text: One side gets hot, the other cold—without any work. Entropy decreases. Did the demon break physics?

twist.text: No. The demon needs information about each molecule's speed. Erasing that information generates entropy.

climax.text: This thought experiment linked thermodynamics to information theory, inspiring modern computer science.

punchline.text: A demon that sorts molecules. Defeated by the cost of forgetting.

quiz.question: Why can't Maxwell's Demon actually violate the second law?
options: Processing and erasing information generates entropy
The door creates too much friction
